steps.title: Isolate r
steps.content:
Starting from $9r = 4(s+t)$, divide both sides by $9$:
$$r = \frac{4(s+t)}{9}$$

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A ($r = \frac{4(s+t)}{9}$): exactly the result of dividing by $9$.
  • Choice B ($r = 4(s+t)$): forgets to divide by $9$.
  • Choice C ($r = 4(s+t) - 9$): subtracts $9$ instead of dividing — wrong inverse operation.
  • Choice D ($r = 4 - s - t - 9$): distributes and subtracts incorrectly.
That confirms A is correct.
